step_by_step_thinking: I fully concur with the final decision. One additional point supporting why the Landau reflex doesn't appear at birth involves its neurological basis. The Landau reflex requires more developed motor control centers in the brainstem and spinal cord compared to simpler reflexes seen earlier in life. Therefore, while other basic reflex arcs function almost immediately upon birth due to less complex neural pathways, the Landau reflex necessitates further maturation before activation. Hence, my agreement remains firm with option A being correct. Final
